audits.excerpt: Enough gold to buy a house in a small town. Enough to buy a magistrate's conscience for a year, if he came cheap.
audits.reason: The bargaining moment lifts into generic purchasing-power comparison instead of staying in purse weight, coin count, and dockside urgency. It reads as narrated commentary rather than pressure Jaina can physically feel.
audits.suggestedFix: Jaina's purse strap bit deeper into her shoulder. Triple passage meant counting out enough stamped gold to fill Dagny's palm twice over.

audits.excerpt: Dagny's brows drew together a fraction. Perhaps she had expected the ritual of argument. Jaina would have argued a month ago. There was no room left for that kind of correctness.
audits.reason: This explains Jaina's shift instead of letting Sylvanas's instant acceptance and Dagny's pause carry it. The last sentence is pure abstract thesis language.
audits.suggestedFix: Dagny's brows drew together a fraction. Jaina said nothing. By the time she found the old objection, Sylvanas was already turning toward the horses.

audits.excerpt: They had carried Stratholme out of the body, mile by mile. From here forward the pursuit belonged to planks, rope, and a captain's appetite for danger.
audits.reason: The line turns the threshold into conceptual summary instead of keeping it in horse heat, leather, and the immediate exchange about stabling. It floats above the harbor work that should be doing the scene's labor.
audits.suggestedFix: The mares' coats had dried white at the harness seams, and the leather under Jaina's hand was still warm. From here the chase had to balance on wet planks and Dagny's tide math.

audits.excerpt: Just facts. The sort of facts that still passed hand to hand after the power to alter anything had failed.
audits.reason: This is essay voice. It steps away from runner, brass tube, and relay procedure to summarize the chapter's politics in abstract terms.
audits.suggestedFix: Cyndia checked the runner's seal, folded the slip once, and tucked it under the thong at her bracer. Harbor. Vessel. Destination. Departure hour. Nothing in it that could bring help.

audits.excerpt: Only an object now, and still she carried it.
audits.reason: The tactile work is already doing the severance. This explanatory tail re-interprets the prop instead of trusting the dead weight at her ribs.
audits.suggestedFix: She touched it through the wool. Smooth surface. Dead chill. It knocked once against her ribs when she turned for the gangplank.

audits.excerpt: The harbor broke apart around her in pieces: dock becoming deck, cliff becoming mast, stable path taking the horses upward while the tide would take them north.
audits.reason: The threshold is rendered as poetic abstraction when the scene needs shipboard geometry and bodily adjustment. The line names transformation instead of materializing it.
audits.suggestedFix: The gangplank thudded under her boots. Behind her, stable hands were already hauling the mares toward the cliff path while the deck shifted under her knees with the tide.

audits.excerpt: Even the light flattened until the world reduced to rope, sail, plank, hand, wave.
audits.reason: This is elegant compression, but too abstract for a grounding pass. It strips away the specific cold and wet textures the paragraph should keep distributing.
audits.suggestedFix: Spray salted the rail. The last harbor smell went out of the air. All she had left was wet rope in one hand, canvas snapping overhead, and the deck lifting under her boots.

audits.excerpt: It hit as an answer. It slammed flat into the bow. A shut door.
audits.reason: The moment leans on metaphor stack instead of the ship's physical failure. The prose should let hull shock, sail behavior, and crew reaction carry the supernatural turn.
audits.suggestedFix: The bow stopped climbing and slammed back into the swell. Canvas cracked. The boom jerked hard enough to wrench the schooner sideways.

audits.excerpt: Then that peculiar clarity that came when you touched a pattern in exactly the place it did not want touched.
audits.reason: This is process-thought language, not embodied perception. It tells the reader how the magic conceptually works instead of what the contact does to Jaina's body.
audits.suggestedFix: Cold needled behind her eyes. The ache in her teeth turned bright. Every time she leaned north, the pressure there tightened and shoved back.

audits.excerpt: North was no longer direction. The source had noticed them.
audits.reason: The pair lands as thesis. It summarizes the change instead of pinning it to repeated failed headings, snapping canvas, and the bow refusing the line.
audits.suggestedFix: Each time the prow nosed north, the pressure stiffened and the canvas snapped back against them.

audits.excerpt: Nothing remained outside the lantern circles except black water showing its teeth in brief white rips and the pale of breaking crests.
audits.reason: The water personification overstates a passage that is otherwise strong on seamanship texture. The white rips are enough without the decorative bite image.
audits.suggestedFix: Outside the lantern glow there was only black water, white rips where crests tore, and spray blowing flat off the tops.

audits.excerpt: The old reflex rose at once: I understand the magic better than you do. True and useless.
audits.reason: This announces the thought in essay form and leans on a process verb. The scene already has enough pressure to show restraint through what Jaina does not say.
audits.suggestedFix: Jaina bit the inside of her cheek and kept her answer to one word. "Understood."

audits.excerpt: The remark had gone where she kept corrections, beside drink and footing and the direction trouble would come from.
audits.reason: The sentence maps Sylvanas into an abstract filing system inside Jaina's head. It should stay with tone, timing, and the concrete pattern of shipboard corrections.
audits.suggestedFix: Sylvanas said it in the same tone she had used for the waterskin and the warning about locked knees, then went back to watching the sail and the wet line crossing behind them.

audits.excerpt: She carried the Wolfheart the way a good field commander carried a retreating line, never pretending things were fine, never giving in to the evidence that they weren't.
audits.reason: The analogy imports battlefield commentary when Dagny's seamanship should stand on its own. It explains competence instead of grounding it in hands, voice, and timing.
audits.suggestedFix: Dagny never wasted a step: eyes on the patched canvas, hand on the mast as she listened, head turning before each order left her mouth.

audits.excerpt: Two paces away, Sylvanas failed by a narrow margin to look unconcerned.
audits.reason: This edges into mind-read shorthand. The prose needs an observable tell instead of a verdict about what expression almost happened.
audits.suggestedFix: Two paces away, Sylvanas's shoulders had gone still and her hand had tightened on the shroud.

audits.excerpt: Cloud lowered. Time lost its edges. Noon may have passed or failed to arrive.
audits.reason: Atmosphere replaces clockable ship experience here. The chapter needs the watch, the light level, or the crew's routines rather than vagueness about time.
audits.suggestedFix: Cloud sank until noon looked like late dusk. The watch glass turned, the lanterns stayed lit, and Jaina stopped trusting the sky to tell her the hour.

audits.excerpt: Spoken with Farstrider authority, in the tone of a quartermaster informing death it would wait its turn.
audits.reason: The sentence swerves into flourish when the scene is stronger in tin cup, broth grease, and Cyndia's dry competence. It over-ornaments a good shipboard beat.
audits.suggestedFix: Cyndia said it dry as inventory and reached for the cup before the deck could tip it into Jaina's lap.

audits.excerpt: The difference, if there was one, would not come from being right. It would come from who could still tell her to stop and be obeyed.
audits.reason: This is the chapter explaining itself. The idea is earned, but here it arrives as essay rather than as the remembered ladder rung, the oilskin, and the obedience her body already gave.
audits.suggestedFix: She saw Sylvanas's hand on the ladder rung again and heard the flat no over the deck noise. The thought stayed because her body had obeyed before her pride had caught up.

audits.excerpt: The pressure she had been following no longer lived only in magic. Air, water, and light had all flattened into one harder world.
audits.reason: This compresses the threshold into abstract atmosphere. Northrend needs to arrive through frost, breath, rigging, and the change in dawn, not a generalized harder world.
audits.suggestedFix: Spray crusted on the shrouds. The air burned colder in her nose. Even the dawn light came off the coast like metal.

audits.excerpt: Half a longboat, charred down one side to naked ribs, lay canted among black rocks above the water mark. Burn pattern old enough to have taken weather.
audits.reason: This undercalls the location brief's burned-fleet evidence. One damaged longboat does not cash out the scale or policy signal that Arthas burned his own extraction route.
audits.suggestedFix: At the far end of the bay, three hulls stood black in the ice, ribs charred bare above the frozen waterline; closer in, a burned longboat lay canted among the rocks where the tide had thrown it.

audits.excerpt: The northward pull in her head no longer resembled a line. It resembled tracks already cut under snow.
audits.reason: The simile blurs a strong arrival read. After the docks, caches, and boot marks, the prose should keep the signal in Jaina's body and the actual shore path instead of drifting into image-work.
audits.suggestedFix: The pull inland had widened. It no longer pricked from one point; it dragged through the whole bay mouth and up the shore path beyond.

audits.excerpt: This time the shaking came from inland.
audits.reason: The attribution is too abstract to audit. The prose needs the visible trigger that turns simple exhaustion into Northrend pressure.
audits.suggestedFix: The dock held steady under her boots, but her hands still shook when she looked past the crates toward the inland trail.
